猜想.摘要: Top1 is unknown and top2 is neutral aromatic, with ICT now third but still a credible named contender. The latest evidence weakens the prior ICT-leading reading rather than strengthening it, because the round-20 microscopic parse reused the round-19 baseline bundle successfully but still reported missing CT/localization and charge-distribution deliverables, so no direct ICT support family was created. The top2 challenger neutral aromatic is indirectly strengthened because it remains unweakened while the intended ICT-discriminating evidence failed to materialize. An additional still-credible named hypothesis remains in coverage debt: ICT and neutral aromatic are both unresolved. The case must remain in portfolio screening, not pairwise contraction, because coverage debt is non-empty and no current named top contender has direct supporting evidence in the hypothesis ledger. The key remaining discriminator is an exact-molecule internal screen that cleanly distinguishes ICT-like charge redistribution from neutral-aromatic/local excitation behavior. With only one round remaining and no room for follow-up, the best action is to stop advancing to contraction, mark the case as blocked by missing decisive evidence, and preserve the current best-available reweighting rather than pretending portfolio closure was achieved.

Portfolio screening remains incomplete at the final round. TICT is directly screened, ESIPT is blocked by capability, and the remaining unresolved named debt is ICT versus neutral aromatic. The latest microscopic artifact-backed parse executed reliably but still failed to deliver the intended molecule-specific CT/localization discriminator, so ICT debt remains open and neutral aromatic also remains unresolved.

Microscopic was the most reliable internal route when mapped to a single supported command, but the final artifact parse still lacked the decisive CT/localization deliverables needed for ICT versus neutral aromatic. Latest evidence: microscopic.extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle executed exactly on round_19_baseline_bundle with no new calculations and reused artifacts only. It reported bounded excited-state output and 46 Mulliken charges at the route level, but also reported missing deliverables for Mulliken charges, CT/localization proxy, and charge distribution at the screening level, so the intended ICT-versus-neutral-aromatic discriminator was not actually discharged. Existing verifier supplementation remains sufficient as close-family context and is mixed rather than molecule-decisive.
